description_long: This is a four-story apartment building with a steep, dark gray hip roof and a single chimney. The facade is painted a light gray or off-white, with regularly spaced rectangular windows on each floor, most of which are grouped in pairs. The building features subtle decorative molding above the windows and along the roofline, giving it a classic, understated appearance. One side of the building is completely flat and windowless, likely designed to adjoin another structure. The main entrance is a single door with a transom window, located at the base of one corner. The overall design suggests a late 19th or early 20th-century European urban architectural style.
description: A four-story, light gray apartment building with a steep dark roof, regular window patterns, and a single main entrance. Its classic facade and windowless side suggest it is designed for dense urban settings.
description_view_0: This view shows the building's side with three floors of paired windows and the steep roof with two skylights.
description_view_1: This view reveals the main entrance door at the base, the chimney, and more paired windows, as well as the roof's slope.
description_view_2: This view displays the completely flat, windowless side of the building and the chimney at the roof's peak.
description_view_3: This view shows the opposite windowless side, with a single skylight visible on the roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical pairs on each floor, with consistent spacing and alignment across the facade. The windowless side walls contrast with the regular window pattern on the main faces.
